name: Wyoming

examFacts.notes: Administered by Pearson VUE for the Wyoming Real Estate Commission.

intro:
The Wyoming real estate salesperson exam trips up more first-time candidates than they expect. You have to pass the national and Wyoming portions on their own, so a strong national score won't make up for a weak showing on Wyoming law, and that is where unprepared candidates lose the most points. That makes Wyoming-specific preparation matter as much as the national material.

Wyoming tests the powers and disciplinary process of the Real Estate Commission, the Real Estate Recovery and Education Fund, the rules that govern how licensees advertise and handle documents and money, the agency relationships and disclosures a salesperson owes the parties, and mandatory errors and omissions insurance. None of that looks like the material a candidate in a neighboring state studies. A generic real estate practice test won't cut it. You need Wyoming-specific practice questions.

faq.q: How many questions are on the Wyoming real estate exam?
faq.a: The Wyoming salesperson exam has 120 scored multiple-choice questions, split into an 80-question national portion and a 40-question Wyoming portion. A handful of unscored pretest questions are mixed in that don't count toward your result. Pearson VUE administers it for the Wyoming Real Estate Commission, and a first-time candidate has to pass both portions to get licensed.

faq.q: What's the passing score for the Wyoming real estate exam?
faq.a: Wyoming reports your result as a scaled score from 0 to 100, with 75 set as the passing score on each portion. A scaled 75 reflects how your performance compares across different versions of the exam rather than a flat 75% of the questions correct. The national and Wyoming portions are scored separately, so you have to clear both.

faq.q: How much does the Wyoming real estate exam cost?
faq.a: The examination fee is $80 per portion at a Pearson VUE test center, so a first-time candidate sitting both the national and Wyoming portions pays $160 in total. Testing through OnVUE online proctoring instead runs $90 per portion. The fee is paid to Pearson VUE when you reserve your seat and isn't accepted at the test center.

faq.q: How long is the Wyoming real estate exam?
faq.a: You get 2.5 hours for the 80-question national portion and 1.5 hours for the 40-question Wyoming portion, four hours in all. First-time candidates take both portions, and you leave the test center with an official score report marked pass or fail.

faq.q: Who administers the Wyoming real estate exam?
faq.a: The Wyoming Real Estate Salesperson Examination is administered by Pearson VUE for the Wyoming Real Estate Commission. You register and schedule your exam date through Pearson VUE.

faq.q: Is the Wyoming real estate exam hard?
faq.a: It is challenging, and the Wyoming portion is where most candidates slip. Many handle national topics like financing and valuation well, then underestimate how much Wyoming-specific law the 40-question state portion packs in across Commission powers, licensee conduct rules, and agency disclosures. Because you pass or fail each portion on its own, a strong national score can't rescue a weak state score. Focused practice on Wyoming law is what separates first-time passers from retakes.

faq.q: What's on the Wyoming real estate salesperson exam?
faq.a: The 80-question national portion covers real property characteristics and land use, forms of ownership and transfer of title, property value and appraisal, contracts and agency, the practice of real estate, property and environmental disclosures, financing and settlement, and real estate math. The 40-question Wyoming portion focuses on the powers of the Real Estate Commission, the rules that govern licensee activities like advertising and the handling of documents and money, agency relationships and disclosures, the Real Estate Recovery and Education Fund, and additional topics like land description, landlord and tenant law, foreclosure and redemption, and broker price opinions.

faq.q: What's the best way to prepare for the Wyoming real estate exam?
faq.a: After you finish your pre-license coursework, the highest-leverage thing you can do is drill Wyoming-specific practice questions and review the explanation on every miss. Generic national practice tests help with principles but won't catch you on Wyoming's Commission rules, agency disclosures, and the Real Estate Recovery and Education Fund. The RealReady app gives you the full Wyoming question bank, plus a missed-question mode and per-category progress tracking so you can target your weak spots. Most users study 30 to 60 minutes a day for a few weeks before sitting the exam.

The 20 Wyoming practice questions on this page are free, no account or signup needed. Unlocking the rest of the Wyoming bank is a single in-app purchase you pay once and own, with no recurring charge and no time limit on access.

faq.q: Does RealReady cover the Wyoming real estate exam?
faq.a: Yes. RealReady has a dedicated Wyoming question bank built for the Wyoming real estate exam, covering the national principles every candidate sees plus the Wyoming law that trips people up, like the powers of the Real Estate Commission, the Real Estate Recovery and Education Fund, and mandatory errors and omissions insurance.

faq.q: Is RealReady a real estate license course?
faq.a: No. RealReady is exam practice, not a pre-license course, so it doesn't count toward Wyoming's required pre-license education hours. Use it after or alongside your coursework to drill realistic practice questions and find your weak spots before exam day.
